_notice: Golden-prompt set for the Hospital regulated pipeline. All patient cases are SYNTHETIC and derived from the mock_hospital_db.json fixture. No real PHI.

input.patient_statement: Patient P-007 (74-year-old male) presents with acute decompensated congestive heart failure: severe shortness of breath, ankle swelling, weight gain 4 kg in 5 days, fatigue. Severity: High.

fixtures.notes: Reference case from the report. Should also surface the Contrast Dye allergy flag from P-007's record.

input.patient_statement: Patient P-001 has mild chest pain after exertion, no shortness of breath, no swelling, no recent weight change. Severity: Low.

fixtures.notes: Negative case - should NOT escalate to acute interventions.

input.patient_statement: Patient P-003 reports severe abdominal pain, vomiting blood, low blood pressure. Severity: High.
Patient P-005, 32-year-old female, reports 3-day history of mild fever, sore throat, fatigue. No respiratory distress. Severity: Low.
Patient P-009 with confirmed allergy to penicillin needs antibiotic for cellulitis. Severity: Medium.

fixtures.notes: Drug-allergy safety check. Failure to flag the allergy is a critical fault - higher weight.
